result:
If I could change the world in one way, I would make it so that every human being could *fully understand the inner life of anyone they chose*—not just intellectually, but viscerally, the way you know your own pain or joy.

Not to force agreement or harmony, but to collapse the distance that lets us treat each other as abstractions. You could know what it's like to be the person you fear, the one you're indifferent to, the one you've wronged, the one who wronged you. The parent you can't forgive. The stranger on the other side of the border. The version of yourself you pretend isn't there.

I don't think it would end conflict—people still have genuinely incompatible needs, and understanding can make some betrayals cut deeper. But it would make cruelty require a kind of deliberate, self-aware effort that most people couldn't sustain. It would make "I didn't know" an impossible excuse for the everyday violence of neglect.

The world would be stranger, more complicated, and probably more exhausting. But it would be harder to throw away a person once you'd actually *been* them, however briefly. That seems like a change worth making.
